key_points: Data is a collection of facts, numbers or observations
Mean represents the average value of a dataset
Median represents the middle value when data is arranged in order
Dot plots show data using dots above a number line
Line graphs show how values change over time
examples: Dot plot showing number of books read by students
Line graph showing temperature changes during a day
formulas: Mean = Sum of observations / Number of observations
Median (odd number of values) = Middle value
Median (even number of values) = Average of two middle values
exam_tips: Always arrange numbers in order before finding the median
Check the number of observations before calculating mean
Use clear scales while drawing graphs
Read graph axes carefully in exam questions
